concerns.text: Energy consumption measured via codecarbon on CPU/GPU does NOT capture the energy efficiency advantage of spiking neural networks. SNN energy benefits arise from sparse, event-driven computation on NEUROMORPHIC HARDWARE (e.g., Loihi, TrueNorth), not software simulation on conventional processors. Claiming 'energy-per-token' savings from a CPU-simulated SNN is scientifically invalid—the metric measures standard CPU power draw, not neuromorphic efficiency. This invalidates the core research claim in the user description.

Assumption: 'Energy consumption measured via codecarbon is independent of model internal activity and represents external power draw; this satisfies the research requirement for independent energy measurement.' This is FALSE. codecarbon measures system power draw which IS directly correlated with computational activity (more spikes/computation = more energy). The validation target (energy) is definitionally determined by the predictor (model activity), creating circular validation. See also User Story 2 acceptance scenario 1.

Training for exactly 3 epochs on WikiText-2 for a 2M parameter transformer is insufficient for convergence. Standard practice requires monitoring validation loss plateau or using early stopping. Without convergence, perplexity comparisons between baseline and spiking models are invalid (one or both may not have learned meaningful representations). This undermines the performance-energy trade-off analysis.

5 independent seeds with paired t-tests may lack statistical power for detecting meaningful differences in energy/perplexity. No power analysis is specified. For small effect sizes common in model comparisons, 5 seeds may yield false negatives. The spec should either justify 5 seeds with power analysis or increase seed count.

FR-004 states 'replace feed-forward sub-layers and/or attention scoring with LIF neurons'—the 'and/or' ambiguity makes the implementation non-reproducible. Specify exactly which components are replaced (FFN only? attention scoring only? both?) to ensure the spiking architecture is well-defined.
